conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a thank you email to a professor after a successful internship program.
org_constraint: Use a professional yet warm tone tailored for an academic audience, avoiding technical jargon.
new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "The internship significantly enhanced my proficiency in utilizing advanced algorithmic paradigms and computational frameworks."
instruction: Write a thank you email to a professor after a successful internship program. Use a professional yet warm tone tailored for an academic audience, avoiding technical jargon. The email must include the phrase "The internship significantly enhanced my proficiency in utilizing advanced algorithmic paradigms and computational frameworks."
response:
thought
Professor.
Successful internship program.
Professional yet warm (academic audience).
Avoid technical jargon (except for the required phrase).
"The internship significantly enhanced my proficiency in utilizing advanced algorithmic paradigms and computational frameworks."

    *   Subject Line: Clear and professional.
    *   Salutation: Formal (Dear Professor [Name]).
    *   Opening: Express gratitude for the opportunity.
    *   Body Paragraph 1: Reflect on the experience (learning, mentorship, specific highlights).
    *   Body Paragraph 2: Include the mandatory phrase and connect it to personal/professional growth.
    *   Closing: Future outlook and final thanks.
    *   Sign-off: Professional closing.

    *   *Subject Line:* Thank You - [My Name] - Internship Completion.

    *   *Salutation:* Dear Professor [Last Name],

    *   *Opening:* I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunity to intern with your research group/department over the past [Duration]. It has been an incredibly rewarding experience.

    *   *Body Paragraph 1 (The Experience):* I truly appreciated your guidance and the chance to work on [Project Name/Topic]. Your mentorship provided me with a deeper understanding of [General Area, e.g., data analysis/research methodology] and allowed me to see how theoretical concepts are applied in a practical setting.

    *   *Body Paragraph 2 (The Required Phrase):* I am particularly grateful for the hands-on experience I gained. **The internship significantly enhanced my proficiency in utilizing advanced algorithmic paradigms and computational frameworks.** This experience has given me a much stronger foundation for my future academic pursuits and career goals.

    *   *Closing:* Thank you again for your time, patience, and support throughout this program. I hope to stay in touch as I continue my studies.

    *   *Sign-off:* Sincerely, [My Name].

    *   *Professional yet warm?* Yes.
    *   *Academic audience?* Yes.
    *   *No technical jargon?* Mostly avoided, except for the required phrase.
    *   *Required phrase included?* Yes.Subject: Thank You - Internship Completion - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for the opportunity to participate in the internship program under your supervision this semester. It has been an incredibly rewarding experience, and I am truly grateful for the guidance and mentorship you provided throughout the duration of the program.

Working within your research environment allowed me to see firsthand how theoretical concepts are applied to solve complex, real-world problems. I particularly appreciated the time you took to share your insights and provide constructive feedback on my progress; your support played a vital role in my professional development.

**The internship significantly enhanced my proficiency in utilizing advanced algorithmic paradigms and computational frameworks.** This experience has not only deepened my understanding of the field but has also given me a much clearer sense of how I can apply these skills in my future academic and professional endeavors.

Thank you again for your time, patience, and for providing such a stimulating learning environment. I hope to stay in touch as I move forward with my studies.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Major/Year]
[Your Phone Number/LinkedIn Profile (Optional)]
